author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, Luke vii. 37, ‘And behold, a woman who was in the city, a sinner,’ etc. On the remission of sins, against the Donatists.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, Luke vii. 37, ‘And behold, a woman who was in the city, a sinner,’ etc. On the remission of sins, against the Donatists.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Since I believe that it is the will of God that I should speak to you on the subject whereof we are now reminded by the words of the Lord out of the Holy Scriptures, I will by His assistance deliver to you, Beloved, a Sermon touching the remission of sins.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For when the Gospel was being read, ye gave most earnest heed, and the story was reported, and represented before the eyes of your heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For ye saw, not with the body, but with the mind, the Lord Jesus Christ “sitting at meat in the Pharisee’s house,” and when invited by him, not disdaining to go.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye saw too a “woman” famous in the city, famous indeed in ill fame, “who was a sinner,” without invitation force her way into the feast, where her Physician was at meat, and with an holy shamelessness seek for health.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: She forced her way then, as it were unseasonably as regarded the feast, but seasonably as regarded her expected blessing; for she well knew under how severe a disease she was labouring, and she knew that He to whom she had come was able to make her whole; she approached then, not to the Head of the Lord, but to His Feet; and she who had walked long in evil, sought now the steps of Uprightness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: First she shed tears, the heart’s blood; and washed the Lord’s Feet with the duty of confession.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: She wiped them with her hair, she kissed, she anointed them: she spake by her silence; she uttered not a word, but she manifested her devotion.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then because she touched the Lord, in watering, kissing, washing, anointing His feet; the Pharisee who had invited the Lord Jesus Christ, seeing He was of that kind of proud men of whom the Prophet Isaiah says, “Who say, Depart far from me, touch me not, for I am clean;” thought that the Lord did not know the woman.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This he was thinking with himself, and saying in his heart, “This man if He were a prophet, would have known what woman this is that” hath approached His feet.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He supposed that He did not know her, because He repelled her not, because He did not forbid her to approach Him, because He suffered Himself to be touched by her, sinner as she was.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For whence knew he, that He did not know her?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what if He did know, O thou Pharisee, inviter and yet derider of the Lord!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou dost feed the Lord, yet by whom thou art to be fed thyself, thou dost not understand.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whereby knowest thou, that the Lord did not know what that woman had been, save because she was permitted to approach Him, save because by His sufferance she kissed His Feet, save because she washed, save because she anointed them?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For these things a woman unclean ought not to be permitted to do with the Feet that are clean?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then had such a woman approached that Pharisee’s feet, he would have been sure to say what Isaiah says of such; “Depart from me, touch me not, for I am clean.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But she approached the Lord in her uncleanness, that she might return clean: she approached sick, that she might return whole: she approached Him, confessing, that she might return professing Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the Lord heard the thoughts of the Pharisee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let now the Pharisee understand even by this, whether He was not able to see her sins, who could hear his thoughts.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then He put forth to the man a parable concerning two men, who owed to the same creditor.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He was desirous to heal the Pharisee also, that He might not eat bread at his house for nought; He hungered after him who was feeding Him, He wished to reform him, to slay, to eat him, to pass him over into His Own Body; just as to that woman of Samaria, He said, “I thirst.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “I thirst”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I long for thy faith.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore are the words of the Lord in this parable spoken; and there is this double object in them, both that that inviter might be cured together with those who ate at the table with Him, who alike saw the Lord Jesus Christ, and were alike ignorant of Him, and that that woman might have the assurance her confession merited, and not be pricked any more with the stings of her conscience.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “One,” said He, “owed five hundred denarii, and the other fifty; He forgave them both: which loved him most?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He to whom the parable was proposed answered, what of course common reason obliged him to answer.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I suppose, Lord, he to whom he forgave most.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Then turning to the woman he said unto Simon, Seest thou this woman?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I entered into thine house, thou gavest Me no water for My feet: she hath washed My feet with tears, and wiped them with her hairs.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou gavest Me no kiss: this woman since the time she came in, hath not ceased to kiss My feet.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: My head with oil thou didst not anoint: but this woman hath anointed My feet with ointment.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore I say, her many sins are forgiven her, for she loved much.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But to whom little is forgiven, the same loveth little.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Here arises a difficulty which must in real truth be resolved, and which requires your fixed attention, Beloved, lest haply my words may not be equal to the removing and clearing of the whole obscurity of it by reason of the stress of time; especially as this flesh of mine exhausted by its heat, now longs to be recruited, and demanding its due, and clogging the eagerness of the soul gives proof of that which is said, “The spirit indeed is willing, but the flesh is weak.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Cause there is for fear, yea great cause for fear, lest by these words of the Lord, there steal over the minds of those who understand them not aright, who indulge their fleshly lusts, and are loth to be brought away from them into liberty, that sentiment which, even as the Apostles preached, sprung up in the tongues of slanderous men, of whom the Apostle Paul says, “And as some affirm that we say, Let us do evil that good may come.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For a man may say, “If ‘he to whom little is forgiven, loveth little;’ and he to whom more is forgiven, loveth more; and it is better to love more, than to love less; it is right that we should sin much, and owe much which we may desire to be forgiven us, that so we may love Him the more who forgiveth us our large debts.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For that woman in the Gospel who was a sinner, in the same proportion as she owed more, loved the more Him who forgave her her debts, as the Lord Himself saith, ‘Her many sins are forgiven her, for she loved much.’

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now why did she love much, but because she owed much?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And afterwards He added and subjoined, ‘But to whom little is forgiven, the same loveth little.’

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Is it not better,” he may say, “that much should be forgiven me, than less, that thereupon I may love my Lord the more”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye see no doubt the great depth of this difficulty; ye see it, I am sure.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye see too my stress of time; yes, this also do ye see and feel.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Accept then a few words.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If I shall not do justice to the magnitude of the question, lay up for a time what I shall say at present, and hold me a debtor for some future time.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Suppose now two men, that by the clearer force of examples ye may think upon what I have proposed to you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: One of them is full of sins, has lived most wickedly for a length of time; the other of them has committed but few sins; they come both to grace, are both baptized, they enter debtors, they go out free; more has been forgiven to one, less to the other.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I ask, how much does each love?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If I shall find that he loves most, to whom the most sins have been forgiven, it is to his greater advantage that he has sinned much, his much iniquity was to his greater advantage, that so his love might not be lukewarm.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I ask the other how much he loves, I find less; for if I find that he too loves, as much as the other, to whom much has been forgiven, how shall I make answer to the words of the Lord, how shall that be true which the Truth hath said, “To whom little is forgiven, the same loveth little”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “See,” a man says, “but little has been forgiven me, I have not sinned much; yet I love as much as he, to whom much has been forgiven.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Dost thou speak truth, or Christ?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Has thy lie been forgiven thee to this end, that thou shouldest fix the charge of lying on Him who forgave thee?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If little has been forgiven thee, thou lovest little.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if but little has been forgiven thee, and thou lovest very much, thou contradictest Him who said, “To whom little is forgiven, the same loveth little.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore I give the more credit to Him, who knoweth thee better than thou dost know thyself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou dost suppose that but little hath been forgiven thee, it is certain that thou lovest but little.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “What then,” says he, “ought I to do?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ought I to commit many sins, that there may be many which He shall be able to forgive me, that I may be able to love more?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It presses me sore, but may the Lord, who hath proposed this saying of truth to us, deliver me out of this strait.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This was spoken on account of that Pharisee who thought that he had either no sins, or but few.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now unless he had had some love, he would not have invited the Lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But how little was it!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He gave Him no kiss, not so much as water for His Feet, much less tears; he did not honour Him with any of those offices of respect, with which that woman did, who well knew what need she had of being cured, and by whom she might be cured.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: O Pharisee, therefore dost thou love but little, because thou dost fondly think that but little is forgiven thee; not because little really is forgiven thee, but because thou thinkest that that which is forgiven is but little.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “What then?” he says; “Am I who have never committed murder, to be reckoned a murderer?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Am I who have never been guilty of adultery, to be punished for adultery?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Or are these things to be forgiven me, which I have never committed?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See: once more suppose two persons, and let us speak to them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: One comes with supplication, a sinner covered over with thorns as a hedgehog, and timid exceedingly as a hare.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the rock is the hedgehog’s and the hare’s refuge.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He comes then to the Rock, he finds refuge, he receives succour.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The other has not committed many sins; what shall we do for him that he may love much? what shall we persuade him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Shall we go against the words of the Lord, “To whom little is forgiven, the same loveth little”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yes, most truly so, to whom little is really forgiven.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But O thou who sayest that thou hast not committed many sins: why hast thou not? by whose guidance?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God be thanked, that by your movement and voice ye have made signs that ye have understood me.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now then, as I think, the difficulty has been solved.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The one has committed many sins, and so is made a debtor for many; the other through God’s guidance has committed but few.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To Him to whom the one ascribes what He hath forgiven, does the other also ascribe what he hath not committed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast not been an adulterer in that past life of thine, which was full of ignorance, when as yet thou wast not enlightened, as yet discerned not good and evil, as yet believed not on Him, who was guiding thee though thou didst not know Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thus doth thy God speak to thee: “I was guiding thee for Myself, I was keeping thee for Myself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That thou mightest not commit adultery, no enticers were near thee; that no enticers were near thee, was My doing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Place and time were wanting; that they were wanting again, was My doing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Or enticers were nigh thee, and neither place nor time was wanting; that thou mightest not consent, it was I who alarmed thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Acknowledge then His grace, to whom thou also owest it, that thou hast not committed the sin.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The other owes me what was done, and thou hast seen forgiven him; and thou owest to me what thou hast not done.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For there is no sin which one man commits, which another man may not commit also, if He be wanting as a Director, by whom man was made.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now then seeing I have resolved this profound difficulty, as best I could in so short a space of time (or if I have not resolved it yet, let me be held, as I have already said, a debtor for the rest); let us now rather consider briefly that question of the remission of sins.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Christ was supposed to be but a man both by him who invited Him, and by them who sat as guests at the table with Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But that woman who was a sinner had seen something more than this in the Lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For why did she all those things, but that her sins might be forgiven her?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: She knew then that He was able to forgive sins; and they knew that no man was able to forgive them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And we must believe that they all, they who were at the table, that is, and that woman who approached to the Feet of the Lord, all knew that no man could forgive sins.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Forasmuch then as they all knew this; she who believed that He could forgive sins, understood Him to be more than man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So when He had said to the woman, “Thy sins are forgiven thee;” they immediately said, “Who is this that forgiveth sins also?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who is this, whom the woman who was a sinner already knew?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou who sittest at the table as if in sound health, knowest not thy Physician; because it may be through a stronger fever thou hast even lost thy reason.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For thus the frantic patient as he laughs is bewailed by those who are in health.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Nevertheless, ye do well to know, and hold fast that truth; yea, hold it fast, that no man is able to forgive sins.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This woman who believed that she could be forgiven by Christ, believed Christ not to be man only, but God also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Who,” say they, “is this that forgiveth sins also?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And the Lord did not tell them as they said, “Who is this?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “It is the Son of God, the Word of God;” He did not tell them this, but suffering them to abide for a while still in their former opinion, He really solved the question which had excited them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He who saw them at the table, heard their thoughts, and turning to the woman, He said, “Thy faith hath made thee whole.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let these who say, “Who is this that forgiveth sins also?” who think me to be but a man, think me but a man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For thee “thy faith hath made thee whole.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Good Physician not only cured the sick then present, but provided also for them who were to be hereafter.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There were to be men in after times, who should say, “It is I who forgive sins, I who justify, I who sanctify, I who cure whomsoever I baptize.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Of this number are they who say, “Touch me not.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yes, so thoroughly are they of this number, that lately, in our conference, as ye may read in the records of it, when a place was offered them by the commissary, that they should sit with us, they thought it right to answer, “It is told us in Scripture with such not to sit,” lest of course by the contact of the seats, our contagion (as they think) should reach to them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See if this is not, “Touch me not, for I am clean.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But on another day, when I had a better opportunity, I represented to them this most wretched vanity, when there was a question concerning the Church, how that the evil in it do not contaminate the good: I answered them, because they would not on this account sit with us, and said that they had been so advised by the Scripture of God, seeing forsooth that it is written, “I have not sat in the council of vanity;” I said, “If ye will not sit with us, because it is written, ‘I have not sat in the council of vanity;’ why have ye entered this place with us, since it is written in the following words, ‘And with them that do iniquity I will not enter in’?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then in that they say, “Touch me not, for I am clean,” they are like to that Pharisee, who had invited the Lord, and who thought that He did not know the woman, simply because He did not hinder her from touching His Feet.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But in another respect the Pharisee was better, because whereas he supposed Christ to be but a man, he did not believe that by a man sins could be forgiven.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There was shown then a better understanding in Jews than heretics.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What said the Jews? “‘Who is this that forgiveth sins also?’

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Does any man dare to usurp this to himself?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What on the other hand says the heretic?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “It is I who forgive, I cleanse, I sanctify.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let not me, but Christ, answer him: “O man, when I was thought by the Jews to be but a man, I gave forgiveness of sins to faith.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: (It is not I, but Christ who answereth thee.)

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And thou, O heretic, mere man as thou art, dost say, “Come, O woman, I will make thee whole.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whereas when I was thought to be but a man, I said, “Go, woman, thy faith hath made thee whole.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They answer, “knowing not,” as the Apostle says, “either what they speak, or whereof they affirm:” they answer and say, “If men do not forgive sins, then that is false which Christ saith, ‘Whatsoever ye shall loose on earth, shall be loosed in heaven also.’”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But thou dost not know why this is said, and in what sense this is said.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Lord was about to give to men the Holy Spirit, and He wished it to be understood that sins are forgiven to His faithful by His Holy Spirit, and not by men’s deserts.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what art thou, O man, but an invalid who hast need of healing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wouldest thou make thyself my physician?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Together with me, seek the Physician.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For that the Lord might show this more plainly, that sins are forgiven by the Holy Spirit, which He hath given to His faithful ones, and not by men’s deserts, after He had risen from the dead, He saith in a certain place, “Receive ye the Holy Ghost;” and when He had said, “Receive ye the Holy Ghost,” He subjoined immediately, “Whosesoever sins ye remit, they are remitted unto them;” that is, the Spirit remits them, not ye.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now the Spirit is God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God therefore remits, not ye.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what are ye in regard to the Spirit?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Know ye not that ye are the temple of God, and the Spirit of God dwelleth in you?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And again, “Know ye not that your bodies are the temples of the Holy Ghost which is in you, which ye have of God?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then God dwelleth in His holy temple, that is in His holy faithful ones, in His Church; by them doth He remit sins; because they are living temples.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But He who remitteth by man, can also remit even without man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He who is able to give by another, hath no less the power to give by Himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To some He gave by the ministry of John.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By whom did He give to John himself?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: With good reason, as God wished to show this, and to attest this truth, when certain in Samaria had had the Gospel preached to them, and had been baptized, and baptized by Philip the Evangelist, one of the seven deacons that were first chosen, they did not receive the Holy Ghost, though they had been baptized.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Tidings were brought to the disciples who were at Jerusalem, and they came to Samaria, in order that they who had been baptized, might by imposition of their hands receive the Holy Ghost.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And so it was; “They came and laid their hands on them, and they received the Holy Ghost.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the Holy Ghost was at that time given in such sort, that He even visibly showed Himself to have been given.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For they who received Him spake with the tongues of all nations; to signify that the Church among the nations was to speak in the tongues of all.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then they received the Holy Ghost, and He appeared evidently to be in them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Which when Simon saw, supposing that this power was of men, he wished it might be his also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What he thought to be of men, he wished to buy of men.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “How much money,” says he, “will ye take of me, that by imposition of my hands the Holy Ghost may be given?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Then Peter says to him with execration, “Thou hast neither part nor lot in this faith.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For thou hast thought that the gift of God may be purchased with money.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thy money perish with thee; and the rest which he spake in the same place suitably to the occasion.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now why I have wished to bring this subject before you, give heed, Dearly Beloved.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It was meet that God should first show that He worketh by the ministry of men; but afterwards by Himself, lest men should think, as Simon thought, that it was man’s gift, and not God’s.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Though the disciples themselves knew this well already.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For there were one hundred and twenty men collected together, when without the imposition of any hand the Holy Ghost came upon them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For who had laid hands on them at that time?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And yet He came, and filled them first.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: After that offence of Simon, what did God do?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See Him teaching, not by words but by things.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That same Philip, who had baptized the men, and the Holy Ghost had not come upon them, unless the Apostles had met together and laid their hands upon them, baptized the officer, that is, the eunuch of queen Candace, who had worshipped in Jerusalem, and returning thence was reading in his chariot Isaiah the Prophet, and understood it not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Philip being admonished went up to his chariot, explained the Scripture, unfolded the faith, preached Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The eunuch believed on Christ, and said when they came unto a certain water, “See water, who doth hinder me to be baptized?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Philip said to him, Dost thou believe on Jesus Christ?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He answered, I believe that Jesus Christ is the Son of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Immediately he went down with him into the water.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When the mystery and sacrament of Baptism had been accomplished, that the gift of the Holy Ghost might not be thought to be of men, there was no waiting, as in the other case, for the Apostles to come, but the Holy Ghost came forthwith.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thus was Simon’s thought destroyed, lest in such a thought he might have followers.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Again, another more wonderful example.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Peter came to Cornelius the centurion, to a Gentile man, uncircumcised: he began to preach Christ Jesus both to him, and to those who were with him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “While Peter was yet speaking,” I do not say, when as yet he had not laid on his hands, but when he had not even yet baptized them, and when they who were with Peter were in doubt whether the uncircumcised ought to be baptized (for there had arisen an offence between the Jews who believed, and those who had been brought to the faith from among the Gentiles, between the Jews, that is, and the Christians who were baptized though uncircumcised), that God might take away this question, “while Peter was speaking, the Holy Ghost came,” filled Cornelius, filled them who were with him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And by this very attestation of so great a thing, as it were a loud voice came to Peter, “Why dost thou doubt of water?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Already I am here.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then let every soul which is to be delivered from her manifold wickedness by the grace of the Lord, to be cleansed as it were in the Church from her filthy prostitution, believe with all assurance, approach the Feet of the Lord, seek His Footsteps, confess in pouring out tears upon them, and wipe them with her hair.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Feet of the Lord are the preachers of the Gospel.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The woman’s hair is all superfluous possessions.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let her wipe the Feet with her hair, yea by all means wipe them, let her do works of mercy; and when she has wiped them, let her kiss them, let her receive peace, that she may have love.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: She has approached to such an one, has been baptized by such an one as the Apostle Paul: from him let her hear, “Be ye followers of me, even as I also am of Christ.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But she has been baptized by another, by one “who seeks his own things, not the things which are Jesus Christ’s:” let her hear from the Lord, “Do what they say, but do not what they do.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So let her assurance be in Him, whether she meet with a good Evangelist, or with one who acts not as he speaks.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For she hears from the Lord with firm assurance, “O woman, go thy way, thy faith hath made thee whole.”
